mathml_representations_list: <mstyle id="custom-group-2" class="custom-group"><mi id="mathml_mi_id_32">δ</mi><msup id="mathml_msup_id_4"><mrow><mi mathvariant="bold" id="mathml_mi_id_33">u</mi></mrow><mi id="mathml_mi_id_34">T</mi></msup></mstyle>

<mstyle id="custom-group-4" class="custom-group"><mi id="mathml_mi_id_40">δ</mi><msup id="mathml_msup_id_5"><mrow><mi mathvariant="bold" id="mathml_mi_id_41">u</mi></mrow><mi id="mathml_mi_id_42">T</mi></msup></mstyle>

concepts_list.name: delta_u
concepts_list.description: Virtual displacement vector used in the principle of virtual work.
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly defined as the test function value vector (virtual displacement) in the principle of virtual work. Appears in the weak form as δu in equations (3) and (7).

concepts_list.name: d_Omega
concepts_list.description: Volume element of integration over the domain Ω.
concepts_list.justification: Appears in integrals over the domain Ω in equations (3) and (8), denoted as dΩ, representing the differential volume element for integration.

concepts_list.name: delta_u_T
concepts_list.description: Transposed virtual displacement vector, used in the dot product with force vectors in the weak form.
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly appears as δu^T in equations (3) and (8), where the transpose of the virtual displacement vector is used in inner products with stress and body force terms.

concepts_list.name: d_Gamma
concepts_list.description: Surface element of integration over the boundary Γ.
concepts_list.justification: Appears in the surface integral over Γ_N in equation (3) and (8), denoted as dΓ, representing the differential surface element for boundary integration.

concepts_list.name: Omega
concepts_list.description: Main computational domain (volume) over which the equilibrium equation is defined.
concepts_list.justification: Referred to as Ω in the initial domain description and in equations (1), (3), and (8), representing the two-dimensional elastic body volume.

concepts_list.name: subscr_T
concepts_list.description: Transpose operator applied to vectors in the weak formulation.
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly used as superscript T in δu^T and N^T in equations (3) and (8), indicating the transpose operation on vectors.

concepts_list.name: C
concepts_list.description: Constitutive matrix that relates stress and strain in the material model.
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the constitutive matrix in equation (3), relating the stress tensor to the strain tensor via the material properties.

concepts_list.name: u
concepts_list.description: Nodal displacement vector representing the actual displacement field in the finite element model.
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the displacement vector in equations (5) and (6), and appears in the strain expression as the primary unknown in the weak form.

concepts_list.name: b
concepts_list.description: Body force vector acting within the domain, such as gravity or inertial forces.
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the body force vector in equation (1), appearing in the weak form as the source term in the integral over Ω.

concepts_list.name: t_macron
concepts_list.description: Imposed traction vector on the Neumann boundary Γ_N.
concepts_list.justification: Defined as the traction on Γ_N in boundary conditions (2), and appears in the weak form as the surface load term on Γ_N.

concepts_list.name: Gamma_N
concepts_list.description: Neumann boundary of the domain, where traction boundary conditions are applied.
concepts_list.justification: Explicitly defined as the boundary Γ_N in equations (2) and (3), where traction conditions are imposed.

concepts_list.name: nabla_sym
concepts_list.description: Symmetric gradient operator used to compute the strain tensor from displacement.
concepts_list.justification: Defined as ∇sym in equation (4) and used in the strain matrix expression as the symmetric gradient of displacement u.
